name: Wrike API
what_it_does: Wrike's REST API for managing tasks, projects, folders, timesheets, workflows, and team collaboration in the Wrike work management platform.
use_cases: Creating and updating tasks and projects programmatically from agent workflows
Reading project status, task assignments, and due dates for automated status reporting
Syncing Wrike tasks with external systems such as CRMs, bug trackers, or CI/CD pipelines
Automating timesheet entry and workload management via API
Triggering Wrike workflows and approvals from agent-driven process automation
not_for: Teams not using Wrike as their primary project management tool
Simple task management without enterprise workflow complexity (use Asana or Trello)
High-frequency bulk data operations — Wrike API has conservative rate limits
best_when: An agent needs to automate work management for teams already invested in Wrike, particularly for enterprise workflows with approvals, custom statuses, and blueprints.
avoid_when: Your organization doesn't use Wrike or you need a simpler, lower-cost project management API integration.

auth.notes: OAuth2 for user-context integrations with fine-grained scopes. Permanent access tokens available for server-to-server agent use. Tokens are account-scoped. Enterprise accounts support service accounts for agent use.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free: up to 5 users, limited storage and integrations, API access included
pricing.paid_tiers: Team $9.80/user/month, Business $24.80/user/month, Enterprise and Pinnacle with custom pricing
pricing.notes: API access is available on all plans. Advanced features like approvals, time tracking, and custom workflows require Business or Enterprise tier. Free tier is functional but limited.

performance.rate_limits: Approximately 600 requests/minute per access token; varies by endpoint

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: API returns HTTP status codes with JSON error bodies containing errorCode and message fields. Error codes are documented but coverage is inconsistent. Rate limit errors include Retry-After header.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: No idempotency key support. Task creation is not idempotent — retries will create duplicate tasks. Agents should query for existing tasks before creating.
known_agent_gotchas: Wrike uses a folder-based hierarchy — tasks must belong to a folder or project, requiring folder ID lookup before task creation
Permanent access tokens require OAuth2 flow to obtain initially — no simple API key issuance in UI
Custom fields are identified by opaque IDs, not human-readable names — requires metadata lookup before use
Wrike's data model distinguishes between folders, projects, and spaces — agents must understand hierarchy
Webhook event types are numerous — agents must subscribe to specific event types or handle unexpected payloads
API responses use a 'data' envelope — all results are nested under the data array even for single-item requests

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. OAuth2 scopes allow limiting agent access to specific resources. Enterprise tier adds IP allowlisting and SAML SSO. Permanent tokens should be treated as secrets.
